title: Invoice line item type catalogue
description: The 8 item types shipped day-1. Each type documents its tax classification (for TaxResolver dispatch), whether it permits negative unit_price (only discount_line + adjustment), whether it counts toward revenue reporting, and the polymorphic reference_type it typically links back to. Extending modules register additional types via #[AsInvoiceLineItemType].

item_types.description: The recurring subscription charge for a Membership period. Links back to membership::Membership via reference_type + reference_id. Feeds the load-bearing marketing::MembershipPurchased signal.
One-time or recurring add-on purchase (extra pass pack, premium feature, tournament entry). Links back to the underlying product via reference_type.
One-time onboarding fee at membership signup. Not recurring. Charged on the first invoice only.
Charged after a past-due invoice reaches a configurable threshold. Optional (tenant may disable in settings).
Ad-hoc manual adjustment (positive or negative) added by admin. Requires internal_notes justification. Feeds an admin-review flag for compliance.
A discrete billable service (private coaching session, custom event registration, apparel purchase). Standalone; not linked to a membership.
Line-item tax breakdown. Populated by CalculateInvoiceTaxJob when the tax module returns a per-jurisdiction breakdown. NOT counted toward revenue (tax is a separate axis on the tax-collected report).
Coupon-driven or admin-applied discount. Carries NEGATIVE unit_price_cents (representing a reduction in subtotal). Links to coupon::CouponRedemption when the discount is coupon-driven.
item_types.notes: counts_toward_revenue=true because the negative amount already REDUCES the revenue total. Excluding it would double-count.

notes: The item_type enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new type = updating this catalogue + updating the InvoiceLineItemType enum + implementing a #[AsInvoiceLineItemType] class.
tax_classification feeds the tax module's TaxResolver — it decides which jurisdiction-specific rate applies. 'exempt' means the tax module returns 0 (no tax computed).
allows_negative_unit_price is a strict check in InvoiceLineObserver.creating. Only discount_line + adjustment may carry negative unit_price_cents; every other item_type is strictly >= 0.
counts_toward_revenue drives the invoice:report --type=revenue query — the SUM aggregation filters WHERE item_type IN (types where counts_toward_revenue=true).
